title: Brume anonymizer config
description: JSON Schema for the user-facing Brume anonymizer config file (commonly named config.yaml). Bound to com.fungle.brume.config.model.AnonymizerConfig.

linked_columns.description: Cross-table column groups that must produce the same anonymized value for the same input.

tables.description: Per-table anonymization rules (one entry per table; columns listed inside).

AnonymizationConfig.description: Cross-table column linkages and per-table, per-column anonymization rules.

json_paths.description: Per-path anonymization rules for this column. Required when type=JSONB; ignored otherwise.

name.description: Column name as it appears in the database schema.

strategy.description: Anonymization strategy to apply to this column.

type.description: Semantic type — required when strategy=FAKE, optional/ignored for HASH/NULLIFY/KEEP/FPE_ID/FPE_UUID. Use JSONB to anonymize specific paths inside a JSON column (see json_paths).

ColumnConfig.description: Anonymization rule for a single column. The 'type' field is required when strategy=FAKE.

table.description: Table name (unqualified, same schema as the rest of the config).

ColumnReference.description: Reference to a column by (table, column) name. Used inside linked_columns.

batch_size.description: Number of rows per INSERT batch on the target. Default: 1000.

chunk_size.description: Number of rows processed in memory per streaming chunk. Default: 10000.

fetch_size.description: JDBC cursor fetch size used during streaming reads from the source. Default: 1000.

fk_depth.description: Maximum FK parent levels to resolve automatically when a row's parent is missing from the extracted set. 1 = direct parents only. Default: 3.

tables.description: Tables to extract. Each entry may carry an optional WHERE filter. Tables not listed are only extracted if they are FK parents of a listed table.

ExtractionConfig.description: Which tables to read from the source database and how — FK propagation depth, batch sizes, and per-table filters.

path.description: JSONPath expression starting with $ — e.g. "$.user.email" or "$.metadata.session.token".

strategy.description: Anonymization strategy applied to that field.

type.description: Semantic type of the targeted JSON field.

JsonPathConfig.description: Anonymization rule for one path inside a JSONB column.

columns.description: Table/column pairs that share this semantic meaning. Minimum two entries for the linkage to be meaningful.

semantic_key.description: Unique identifier for this cross-table linkage. Used as part of the substitution-dictionary key. Example: "user_email".

LinkedColumnsConfig.description: Group of columns across tables that must receive the same anonymized value (e.g. users.email and audit_logs.user_email).

SemanticType.description: Semantic type of a column value — drives fake data generation (FAKE) or masking pattern (MASK). JSONB requires a non-empty json_paths list.

Strategy.description: Anonymization strategy. FAKE = deterministic fake (requires type). MASK = partial masking. HASH = SHA-256 one-way. NULLIFY = null. KEEP = copy unchanged. FPE_ID = format-preserving for numeric IDs. FPE_UUID = format-preserving for UUIDs.

columns.description: Ordered list of per-column anonymization rules.

TableAnonymizationConfig.description: Anonymization rules for all columns of one table.

filter.description: Optional SQL WHERE clause snippet, without the WHERE keyword. Null or blank = all rows. Security validation: see README § Configuration > extraction.

table.description: Unqualified table name (the schema is configured separately via replication.schema in application.yaml).

TableExtractionConfig.description: One table to extract, with an optional WHERE filter applied during the source read.

anonymization.description: Anonymization rules applied to extracted rows before they reach the target.

extraction.description: Source-side extraction rules: which tables to read and with which filters.
